## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey guys, we're going to talk about an interesting topic and that is removing clients from Upwork. So these are people who you already have on the Upwork platform. They're people who you know you connected with and whether they're a current client or they're about to be a client and you take them off of the platform for them to pay somewhere else whether that's via Stripe or PayPal or something, not via Upwork. Most commonly it's for two main reasons that you would remove people from the platform but please watch till the end of the video before you jump and do that. So the two main reasons to do that are to avoid the fees and also avoid potential bad reviews because let's face it, the reality is that most situations you're going to run into with clients over time there's going to reach a period or at least a phase where the relationship might not be perfect and there is a chance that they can end that contract and give you a bad review and if you've made thousands or tens of thousands with this client and you get a bad review that's going to look really bad so that's the last thing you want. So those are the two most logical reasons you would remove people from Upwork. So I want to share my experience with this because in our agency we currently have over 10,000 recurring income that we've brought off of the Upwork platform. Don't share that with Upwork. And I want to share my experience with that because originally the idea was yeah we'll get the client maybe service them for a month get a good review bring them off the platform. And generally that's a good strategy but there's two reasons that you probably shouldn't do it and I'm going to recommend against doing it and moving forward I'm trying to flow everything through Upwork so a couple things I want to address. First thing is if Upwork catches you doing it they will ban you period. There's no warning there's no hey you can't bring people off it they're just going to ban you. It doesn't matter if you've made $50,000 or $100,000 on the Upwork platform if you take someone off of Upwork platform and they catch you doing it you will be banned to the extremity if you do that and even if you communicate it as a request with a client let's say you have a client on Upwork that's paying you through Upwork and you say in a Slack channel or an email that you would like to flow out of Upwork and maybe that relationship doesn't go so well and they report you to Upwork there's a very good chance that they will ban you just outright permanent ban you're gone your account is deleted so obviously that's a big concern and so you have to be really really really careful with the approach if you're going to remove people from the Upwork platform right. So you know just be careful with it you can do it and I've done it but be very careful and the reason that I'm recommending against it is because the likelihood to get banned but more so that it's so important to have a large amount of income you know made on

## [03:02 - 04:48]

the Upwork platform sure you do eat fees you do eat a little bit of fees but just charge 5% 10% higher so so you can eat those fees comfortably and not have to worry too much about it I know it can sound rough at first because you're like wow they're about to charge me you know 20% if it's a small gig but it's only 20% until $500 as we spoke about the fees before so be very careful if you communicate you want to bring it off the platform another thing and a reason to have people on the platform is once you become top rated Upwork will actually give you the ability to bring clients onto the Upwork platform and pay zero fees for the lifetime of that client so if you take someone who's paying you $4,000 a month and you bring them onto Upwork to pay you $4,000 a month you will not pay 5% on that you will not be 10% 20% you'll pay nothing on that if you're top rated so that's a big benefit of bringing people on the platform so my recommendation is to keep people on the platform but be wary with it because if you feel like the relationship with the client is a little bit shaky and you're like I might get a 3 or 4 star review with this it might be worth it to take a little slick approach and try and remove that from the platform so you can you know even if you have intentions of salvaging that relationship I'm still to just avoid the potential bad review because Upwork is so predominantly you know reputation based so it's really important not to get bad reviews so all I'm saying is whether you decide to do it or not if you do do it be careful if you don't do it and you keep everyone on the platform you're going to be safe but of course make sure you're strict with who you accept so that you can minimize the effect or the chance of getting you know less than 5 star reviews.
